text:
There once was a frog who wanted to build a tower. He gathered all sorts of things: pebbles, twigs, lint balls -- you name it. There were no bricks, though. That was the problem.

Every evening he would sit on his dewy rock perch collecting his treasure pile. After weeks of collecting, he began to construct. He filled his tower with the finest lint balls he'd gathered.

It started small, just a sturdy base with lint balls. Then he added rungs. The branches didn't hold lint well, so he twisted some hairpins into little beams.

The night was dark, so he lit lanterns with bits of candle wax. When he was finished?

He tipped it over. It collapsed with a clatter!

Well, he had to start over. This time he made sure to use bricks.

Each time he added a brick. There were gaps!

After a few more nights, he decided that was close enough. The tower was sturdy. There
